notation_signal: launch token (TOE, CLIP, and the generic SET)
evidence: TOE and CLIP name a surface; the generic SET launch names none
notes: a SET-launched movement leaves its entry surface unspecified, so one SET notation covers several entry surfaces

evidence: [DEL] present in 97.0% of the active corpus; every terminal names its surface
notes: the endpoint is the best-represented single coordinate

notation_signal: SAME / OP prefix on each dex; SAME/OP for an either-side reading
collapse_role: one-to-many source (SAME/OP), and absolute value not recoverable
evidence: SAME/OP written on every dex, but the reference foot it is measured against is never named; 40 rows write an explicit SAME/OP ambiguity
notes: the relative side is kept; the absolute (left/right) frame is dropped, so the side is recoverable only relative to an unrecorded frame

notation_signal: IN / OUT, plus rotational SWIRL / WHIRL and BACK
evidence: IN and OUT dominate; SWIRL 8.7%, WHIRL 4.8%, BACK 7.5% of the corpus
notes: direction is richer than binary and is fully written

notation_signal: [BOD] plus a named body event (SPIN, DUCK, DIVE)
evidence: [BOD] in 59.0% of the corpus; SPIN 30.5%, DUCK 18.9%, DIVE 4.2%
notes: body events are explicitly written where present; WEAVE and ZULU tokens do not exist (0 rows), a known token gap
the paradox modifier is written where present; its scoring is a separate, unresolved question outside representation

evidence: (no plant while) 19.4%, (plant) 1.6%; most rows carry no stance marker and default
notes: stance is written for the marked minority; elsewhere it is a default, so distinct stances can collapse

notation_signal: none; inferable only from the dex tokens under a transition rule
evidence: no token exists; the validation found carriage inferable in 100% of two-dex rows but explicit in 0%
notes: the state the movement passes through is not written; two movements differing only in carriage share one notation

evidence: the reference foot is absent from 100% of rows (validation)
notes: the absolute (left/right) frame is dropped as laterality (see compression); the frame-transport relation between dexes is also lost, which does affect composition
which leg supports is never named; dropped as laterality
which leg circles is never named; dropped as laterality

evidence: (back) 27.9%, (front) 3.5%; continuous rotational phase is not written
notes: coarse facing is written; fine phase between events is not

evidence: 107 of 395 two-dex rows are repeated dexes, all written as two literal events (85 sequential, 22 co-articulated)
notes: at the operational layer a repeat is two events; a count-operator reading exists only at the naming layer, a controlled one-to-many
the sequence-versus-layer distinction is written; the scoring of the layered case is unresolved, but that is scoring, not representation

notation_signal: the count of scored brackets ([DEX] [BOD] [XBD] [UNS] [PDX] [XDEX], with [KICK] non-scoring)
evidence: ADD equals the scored-bracket count; stored separately as adds / computed_adds
notes: scoring is not a token but is directly derivable from the bracket structure; it is the notation's organizing principle
the far-receiver scoring condition has its own rare token

evidence: no token exists in the notation
notes: vertical phase and trajectory are physical detail the notation does not attempt to carry
